evals.prompt: Before I consolidate ticket-writing guidance across the project-advisor skills, run a CRAP analysis focused on plugins/project-advisor/skills and tell me what I should de-risk first.
evals.expected_output: A focused report on cross-skill authority, handoff, packaging, and verification risks tied to the project-advisor skill files and references.
expectations: Uses the CRAP report structure with an executive summary and findings.
Keeps the analysis focused on plugins/project-advisor/skills rather than drifting into unrelated repo areas.
Identifies a concrete contract, authority, packaging, or verification risk inside the project-advisor skill surface.
Recommends one specific first mitigation tied to the project-advisor skill scope.

evals.prompt: I want to tighten the security-guard policy around blocked follow-ups. Run a CRAP analysis focused on plugins/security-guard and tell me which risks I need to de-risk before changing policy behavior.
evals.expected_output: A module-scoped report that names risky policy, event, test, and rollout boundaries inside security-guard rather than drifting into a repo-wide review.
expectations: Uses the CRAP report structure with an executive summary and findings.
Keeps the analysis centered on plugins/security-guard instead of broad repo packaging concerns.
Names a policy-parity, host-coverage, or verification-boundary risk directly related to tightening blocked follow-ups.
Recommends a concrete first mitigation such as freezing a contract or adding shared fixtures for blocked and allowed cases.

evals.prompt: Give me a bounded repo-level CRAP analysis for this monorepo and tell me the safest first hardening move for future plugin work.
evals.expected_output: A bounded repository-level report that samples the highest-signal plugin surfaces, cites evidence, and prioritizes a small number of concrete change-risk findings.
expectations: Uses the CRAP report structure with an executive summary and findings.
Starts from shared plugin inventory, packaging, registration, installer, or verification surfaces for future plugin work.
Recommends one repo-level validator, manifest, or release gate as the first hardening move.
Keeps the headline findings bounded to a small set instead of expanding into a broad plugin-by-plugin audit.
